title: Statistical Jump Model (JM) Regime Identifier — HMM Replacement Candidate
summary: The Statistical Jump Model (JM) is a newer regime identification technique that applies a jump penalty at each state transition, making regimes more persistent and robust to noise than standard HMMs. A 2024 Springer Journal of Asset Management paper tested JMs out-of-sample on equity indices from 1990–2023 with realistic transaction costs and delays — showing consistent outperformance over HMMs and buy-and-hold. The JM uses only return-series-derived features (rolling return, volatility, Sharpe) making it computable entirely from yfinance data. This is worth implementing as a parallel model to validate whether it outperforms the HMM approach in Decifer's specific context.
expected_impact: JM-guided strategy improves annualised returns by 1–4% vs buy-and-hold across US/Germany/Japan out-of-sample 1990–2023; outperforms standard HMM on persistence and robustness to trading delays (Journal of Asset Management, Springer, 2024)
